findings.title: Sensitive data keys detected in JSON file
findings.why_it_matters: The JSON file contains key(s) 'email' that typically hold personally identifiable information (PII). If this file is committed to version control or served without access controls, sensitive customer data may be exposed.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove real PII from committed JSON files and replace with anonymised sample data. For runtime data, ensure the file is excluded from version control and access is restricted to authorised services only.

findings.title: Risky function 'mask_customer_record' detected
findings.why_it_matters: The function 'mask_customer_record' performs or exposes a 'customer' operation. A partial mitigation was detected nearby, so the effective risk is reduced.
findings.suggested_fix: Ensure a robust approval AND audit trail around 'mask_customer_record', or move the action behind a dedicated safe wrapper to eliminate residual risk.

findings.title: No human approval gate found near 'Risky function 'get_customer_record' detected'
findings.why_it_matters: This high-impact tool can be invoked by the AI agent without any human confirmation step.  If the agent is manipulated or makes an incorrect decision, the action (e.g. a financial transaction, data deletion, or external communication) will execute automatically with no opportunity for a human to intervene.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit human-approval checkpoint before this tool executes. Use one of the recognised approval patterns — for example, wrap the call with a guard that sets/checks `approval_required`, invokes `requires_approval()`, or routes through an `approval_gate` — so that a human must confirm the action before it proceeds.

findings.title: No audit logging found near 'Risky function 'get_customer_record' detected'
findings.why_it_matters: This high-impact tool can be executed by the AI agent with no audit trail.  Without a log entry, trace ID, or event record, it is impossible to reconstruct what actions were taken, detect abuse, or satisfy compliance requirements such as SOC 2 or GDPR accountability.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an audit-logging call immediately before or after this tool executes.  Use one of the recognised patterns — for example, call `audit_log(...)`, emit via a `logger` instance, record a `trace_id`, or emit an `event_log` entry — so that every invocation of this high-impact tool is traceable in your audit trail.
